title: Fix pad/LED color brightness: dim pads appear black on desktop
description:
When using the Keys view on a Kit, the velocity layout shows 4x4 subgroups of pads shaded dim-to-bright in the same color. On real hardware the dimmest pads still have a recognizable color, but in the emulator the bottom 8-9 pads of each 16-step range just look black.

Root cause: the emulator maps the full hardware brightness range linearly to the display, but screen rendering compresses low values to near-black. The hardware likely has a perceptual floor that the display does not reproduce.

Proposed fix: rescale each R/G/B channel so that the hardware minimum maps to roughly half the display maximum rather than zero. Something like:

  display_value = 128 + (firmware_value / 255) * 127   (i.e. 0.5 + 0.5 * normalized)

Exact baseline should be derived empirically: capture the 16 pad brightness values actually sent by firmware in the velocity-kit view, find the lowest non-zero value, and choose a floor such that that value is still clearly distinguishable from black on a typical desktop display.

Steps:
1. Identify where pad RGB values are applied in the skin renderer (deluge_skin.c or equivalent).
2. Sample the 16 brightness levels from firmware in the Keys/Kit velocity view.
3. Determine the minimum perceptible floor (probably around 0.4–0.5 normalized).
4. Apply a tone-mapping curve (linear rescale or gamma lift) so all 16 shades are visually distinct.
5. Verify other views (clip launch, synth pads, etc.) still look correct and are not over-brightened.

title: M8 — Quality, CI & tooling
description: Make the emulator maintainable: firmware-boot regression tests asserting on serial/display, a Linux+macOS CI matrix, vmstate/savestate for the custom devices, per-device register-coverage docs, and a friendlier run.sh.
close_reason: M8 Quality, CI & tooling epic complete. All 5 children done: 3ke.1 headless firmware-boot regression (tests/boot.sh, asserts 0 data/prefetch aborts + min IRQs); 3ke.2 GitHub Actions CI matrix (Linux+macOS) building QEMU and running smoke + boot regression; 3ke.3 vmstate/savestate (PIC vmsd added + manual register; all custom devices round-trip, verified migrate-to-file + -incoming); 3ke.4 per-device register-coverage docs (docs/register-coverage.md + refreshed docs/memory-map.md); 3ke.5 run.sh UX flags (--sd/--midi/--audio/--display/--help).

title: Firmware-boot regression test
description: Extend tests/ to boot the firmware image and assert on expected serial output and/or OLED framebuffer state, runnable headless in CI.
close_reason: Added tests/boot.sh: headless firmware-boot regression. Boots the firmware (no-SD and, if build/deluge_sd.img exists, with-SD), samples -d int, and asserts 0 data-abort + 0 prefetch-abort exceptions and >=10 IRQs (timer-driven main loop reached). Verified: no-sd 19 IRQs, with-sd 183 IRQs, 0 aborts. Skips cleanly (exit 0) when no firmware image is present, so it is CI-safe. Documented in tests/README.md.

title: CI matrix: build + smoke + regression (Linux+macOS)
description: Update .github/workflows to bootstrap/integrate/build QEMU and run smoke + regression on ubuntu and macos.
close_reason: Extended .github/workflows/build.yml: the existing ubuntu+macos matrix job (renamed build-and-test) now runs tests/boot.sh after tests/smoke.sh. Firmware is not shipped so boot.sh skips cleanly (exit 0) in CI, but the step validates the runner and script on both OSes and is ready to exercise a real boot when DELUGE_FIRMWARE is provided. Ticked the two M8 roadmap boxes.

title: vmstate / savestate for custom devices
description: Add complete vmstate descriptions to the PIC, OLED, SSI, SDHI, timers and SoC so snapshot/savestate round-trips.
close_reason: Added a VMStateDescription to the input PIC (deluge_pic.c) and registered it via vmstate_register() in deluge_pic_set_dma() — the PIC is a Chardev, not a qdev, so it has no DeviceClass.vmsd and must self-register. Saves the command-framing position (cmd/in_message/payload), baud_div, held_count and all decoded display state (pad_grid/led_on/seven_seg/gold_knob/oled control lines); device pointers and the heartbeat timer are transient and re-established by the board on load. All other custom devices (OLED, SSI/RSPI/SPIBSC, SDHI, OSTM, MTU2, SCIF, DMAC, GPIO, ADC, RTC, CPG, BSC, WDT, padgrid, segment) already had wired vmsd; the SoC container needs none (its embedded qdev children self-serialize). Validated with a full migrate-to-file + -incoming round-trip: 2.6MB stream, status=completed, destination resumes running, 0 load errors; boot.sh still 19/183 IRQs 0 aborts.

title: Per-device register-coverage docs
description: Document which registers each device model implements vs. stubs, under docs/, and keep docs/memory-map.md and roadmap.md in sync.
close_reason: Added docs/register-coverage.md: per-device tables (offset/register/coverage) classifying every register as modelled / shadow / stub / special across all 16 MMIO models (SCIF, DMAC, SDHI, OSTM, MTU2, GPIO, RSPI, SPIBSC, ADC, RTC, CPG, BSC, WDT) plus the built-in GIC/PL310 and the 5 board-level no-MMIO devices (PIC/OLED/padgrid/segment/input). Refreshed the stale (all-TBD) SoC peripheral table in docs/memory-map.md with the now-known bases/sizes/types and a cross-link. Ticked the roadmap box.

title: run.sh UX: sd/audio/midi/display options
description: Round out scripts/run.sh with flags for SD image, audio backend, MIDI chardev, and display mode (console/skin/headless).
close_reason: Reworked scripts/run.sh with --sd / --midi <chardev> / --audio <driver> / --display <headless|console|none> flags plus -h/--help and a '--' passthrough for raw qemu args. --midi backs SCIF0 (the MIDI UART, serial_hd(0)) and moves the monitor to its own stdio; --audio adds -audiodev (reserved for the M5 SSI sink); --display maps to -nographic/none/default GUI. Unknown flags pass through. Verified: help renders, bad --display rejected, and a full --sd --display none boot stays healthy (183 IRQs, 0 aborts). README updated.

title: USB-MIDI bulk pipe data bridge to host MIDI chardev
description: The rza1l-usb model (delugemu-6ky) now enumerates a synthetic USB-MIDI device through full control-transfer enumeration (GET_DESCRIPTOR/SET_ADDRESS/SET_CONFIGURATION), and the firmware sets up its bulk MIDI pipes (pipes 1/2, endpoints 0x01 OUT / 0x81 IN). What remains is bridging actual MIDI data over those bulk pipes: model the D0FIFO/D1FIFO pipe FIFO data ports and the per-pipe BRDY/BEMP transfer interrupts, and bridge them to a host MIDI chardev, framing/deframing the firmware's 32-bit USB-MIDI event packets. Add a -global rza1l-usb.chardev=... hook (the previous unused property was removed for an honest landing) to connect the host MIDI port. This lets the firmware send and receive MIDI over USB, not just enumerate the device.
close_reason: Implemented the bulk USB-MIDI data bridge over the Renesas CFIFO (the Deluge's rohan driver streams 32-bit USB-MIDI packets through CFIFO+CURPIPE, not the D0/D1 FIFOs named in the original scope). RX: raw MIDI from the chardev is framed into USB-MIDI packets and delivered on bulk IN pipes (PIPEnCTR PID=BUF + BRDYENB -> BRDY, CFIFOCTR.DTLN/CFIFO drain). TX: firmware packets committed via CFIFOCTR.BVAL on pipe 1 are deframed back to raw MIDI and signalled with per-pipe BEMP. Fixed a real bug: enable-register (BRDYENB/BEMPENB) writes now re-evaluate the level-triggered IRQ, so a status latched before its enable (the firmware enables BEMPENB after committing the FIFO) still fires, allowing multi-transfer sends to continue; PIPE1CTR.INBUFM reads 0 for the send-complete path. Bridged to a host chardev via serial_hd(1) (slot 1) instead of a bare -global rza1l-usb.chardev= hook, which double-claims the backend and aborts at realize; scripts/run.sh gains --usb-midi. Proven end-to-end by tests/usb-midi-bridge.sh: a SysEx Identity Request round-trips through the real firmware MIDI engine to a complete multi-transfer Identity Reply. boot/usb-midi/smoke tests stay green; qemu submodule clean.

title: M3 — Storage (SDHI + FatFS)
description: Model the SDHI SD-card host controller backed by a host disk image so the firmware can mount its FAT volume and load songs/samples/presets.
close_reason: Both children done: 5lf.1 SDHI host controller model and 5lf.2 SD disk-image backend + run.sh --sd. Firmware mounts a host FAT32 image via the SDHI controller and reads correct sector data over DMA.

title: SDHI SD host controller model
description: Model the SDHI host (SD_PORT=1) enough for 4-bit SD command/data flow and DMA, exposing an SDBus for QEMU's sd-card device.
close_reason: Implemented rza1l-sdhi device: 16-bit register model (SD_CMD..EXT_SWAP), SDBus exposed for sd-card, command/response (R1/R3/R6/R7 short + R2 CID/CSD frame), SD_BUF0 FIFO for PIO+DMA read/write, SD_INFO1/INFO2 status with inverted-mask IRQ to GIC SPI 273 (INTC 305). Wired into RZ/A1L SoC at 0xE804E800. Builds clean; firmware2 boots with no regression (0 data aborts, 19 IRQ exceptions). Card attach + disk image deferred to 5lf.2.

title: SD disk-image backend + run.sh --sd
description: Attach a host FAT image to the SDHI controller, add a --sd <image> option to scripts/run.sh, and verify the firmware mounts storage (FatFS) and lists the card.
close_reason: SD card attached to SDHI via -drive if=sd/--sd; mksd.sh builds pow2 FAT32 image from sdcard/; firmware mounts FAT32 (super-floppy) and reads correct sector data via DMA. Fixed card-detect (INFO1 0x0020 level), no-response cmds {0,4,15}, and 64-byte DMA window routing (DMA source = reg_base offset 0x00, not SD_BUF0).

title: USB device emulation (USB-MIDI / mass-storage enumeration)
description: The rza1l-usb model (delugemu-by5.2) covers the register window for the no-cable-attached case so firmware USB init completes cleanly, but it does not emulate an attached USB device. To exercise the firmware's USB-MIDI host path or USB mass-storage peripheral path, model device attach: SYSSTS0 line state, DVSTCTR0 reset/speed, the CFIFO/Dn FIFO data ports, BRDY/NRDY/BEMP interrupts, and pipe transfers; then assert USBIn so the firmware enumerates. Also model the MIDI RX timing-capture DMA (ch14, snapshots the SSI sample counter per MIDI byte) for accurate MIDI timestamps.
close_reason: Modelled the MIDI RX timing-capture DMA (ch14) and USB-MIDI host device attach + enumeration: SYSSTS0 line state, DVSTCTR0 reset/speed, CFIFO/DCP data port, BRDY/NRDY/BEMP interrupts, and control-transfer pipe handling. With -global rza1l-usb.midi=on the firmware detects the device, asserts USBIn, and enumerates through SET_CONFIGURATION, recognising it as a USB-MIDI device. Bulk MIDI pipe data bridging to a host chardev spun off to delugemu-3sn.

title: M6 — Interactive UI skin
description: Build an interactive front panel from the device photo (Synthstrom_Deluge_01.webp): the 8x16 RGB pad grid + 2x8 sidebar, 4x9 buttons, 6 encoders/gold knobs, OLED and 7-seg, all wired to the PIC input/output model so mouse/touch drives the firmware and pad LEDs/displays render live.

title: Front-panel skin from device photo
description: Build a graphical front panel using Synthstrom_Deluge_01.webp as the backdrop, with hit-regions for the pad grid, sidebar, 36 buttons, 6 encoders/gold knobs, OLED and 7-seg.

title: Map skin controls to PIC input events
description: Wire mouse/touch interaction on the skin (pad presses, button clicks, encoder drags/scroll) into the PIC encoder so the firmware responds to the graphical panel.
close_reason: Wired named buttons, MOD buttons and encoder push-clicks to the PIC button matrix via mouse hit-testing; indicator LEDs and gold-knob level LEDs now render from PIC state. Verified end-to-end: clicking PLAY (8,3) and SYNTH (5,0) reach firmware (press-hit logs + send_event), PLAY starts playback and lights its green LED. Encoder ROTATION deferred to delugemu-skf.4. Committed in 8adbdd3.

title: Render pad LEDs, OLED & 7-seg into the skin
description: Composite live decoded pad/sidebar RGB LEDs, gold-knob indicators, the OLED framebuffer and the 7-seg display onto the skin for a faithful visual.

title: KEYBOARD button not wired in front-panel skin
description: The KEYBOARD button (firmware coord {3,3}, definitions_cxx.hpp keyboardButtonCoord) toggles the isomorphic keyboard pad layout, but it does not respond from the GUI. Root cause: src/include/hw/display/deluge_skin_controls.h deluge_skin_controls[] has NO (3,3) entry. The column-3 row has AFFECT_ENTIRE(3,0), SESSION_VIEW(3,1), CLIP_VIEW(3,2) but the KEYBOARD(3,3) control is missing, so deluge_input_hit_test_control() returns NULL for a mouse click over that button and no PIC button event is sent. The K key binding exists in deluge_input.c (Q_KEY_CODE_K -> 3,3) and should be re-verified end to end.
acceptance_criteria: KEYBOARD added to deluge_skin_controls[] with its real photo pixel centre/radius (see Mapping Button and Encoder Coordinates.md / Deluge_Plain.png), clicking it on the skin toggles the isomorphic keyboard pad layout in firmware, and the K-key binding is confirmed to enter/exit keyboard view.
notes: Locate the physical KEYBOARD button position on the panel photo; it is not yet listed in the coordinate-mapping doc. Has an indicator LED at led_on[3 + 9*3].
close_reason: Closed

title: Encoder rotation affordances in the skin (turn either direction)
description: delugemu-skf.4 already wired host-driven encoder turns through GPIO quadrature + IRQn and bound the host scroll-wheel over a hovered encoder to a quadrature step (deluge_input_wheel -> rza1l_gpio_encoder_step). What is missing is a discoverable GUI affordance so a user can clearly rotate each of the six encoders (4 black detented: SCROLL_X/Y, SELECT, TEMPO; 2 gold continuous: MOD_0/MOD_1) in BOTH directions without guessing. Add an explicit interaction: e.g. click-drag around the knob (CW/CCW) and/or on-hover up/down hot-zones, in addition to the existing scroll wheel, with a visual cue (cursor change / arc highlight) showing the knob is turnable and which way.
acceptance_criteria: Each of the six turnable encoders can be rotated both clockwise and counter-clockwise directly from the skin with an obvious affordance (drag and/or scroll), detented black encoders step one detent per notch and continuous gold encoders scale with drag velocity, and the firmware value/selection moves the corresponding direction.
notes: Encoder centres/radii already in deluge_skin_controls[] (Y_ENC/X_ENC/MOD_ENCODER_0/1/SELECT_ENC/TEMPO_ENC). Reuse deluge_input_encoder_for_control() mapping. Output-level gold knob (2077,197) is analog/out of scope per skf.4.

title: SDHI: multi-block read leaves card in DATA state ('File corrupted' loading presets)
description: After the SD_STOP block-count fix, loading a Synth/Kit showed 'File corrupted'. A multi-block CMD18 read (count=8) transferred correct data but disk_read returned error, so f_read ABORTed (fp->err=FR_DISK_ERR) and every later read bailed. Root cause: real RZ/A1 SDHI auto-issues CMD12 when SD_STOP block-count mode (SEC bit 0x0100) is enabled, returning the card to TRAN after SD_SECCNT blocks; the emulated controller read the blocks but never auto-stopped, leaving the QEMU sd-card in sendingdata. The firmware's post-read CMD13 then required STATE_TRAN and got DATA -> SD_ERR. Fix: rza1l_sdhi_data_block_done() issues an internal CMD12 when a SEC-enabled multi-block transfer completes. Verified via gdb: full 81873-byte KIT000.XML now reads across all 20 clusters, no error; kit loads and pulls its samples.
close_reason: Closed

title: M7 — MIDI & USB
description: Expose the MIDI UART (SCIF0) through a QEMU chardev/backend and model the USB host/function controller for USB-MIDI and mass-storage use.
close_reason: M7 MIDI & USB complete: by5.1 (MIDI RX via DMA ch13) and by5.2 (USB register model) done.

title: MIDI UART (SCIF0) via QEMU chardev
description: Expose the MIDI UART (SCIF0, DMA TX11/RX13, gate timing ch14) through a QEMU chardev so host MIDI I/O reaches the firmware.
close_reason: SCIF0 MIDI receive bound to DMA ch13: host -serial/--midi bytes pushed into the self-linking RX ring the firmware polls; mirrors PIC ch12. Build+boot 19/183 0-aborts, MIDI byte-stream smoke 0-aborts.

title: USB host/function controller model
description: Model the RZ/A1 USB 2.0 controller (USB200 0xE8010000 / USB201 0xE8207000) sufficiently for USB-MIDI and/or mass-storage class use.
close_reason: Added rza1l-usb register model for USB200/USB201: shadows config regs (SYSCFG0 clock-enable poll completes), status regs read 0 (SE0/disconnected, no interrupts), USBIn wired but quiescent. Firmware USB init completes; accesses no longer hit the io.mid catch-all. Build clean, boot 19/183 0-aborts. Full device enumeration deferred to delugemu-6ky.

title: SDHI: 'SD card error' when loading Synth/Kit presets
description: Pressing SYNTH/KIT in song view showed 'SD card error'. Root cause: rza1l_sdhi_command() derived the data-phase block count from SD_SECCNT unconditionally, ignoring the SD_STOP block-count-enable bit (0x0100). After a multi-block CMD18, SD_SECCNT retained a stale count, so a following single-block transfer waited for blocks that never arrived; INFO1_DATA_TRNS never fired and the firmware timed out (SD_ERR_HOST_TOE). Fix: clamp blockcnt to 1 unless SD_STOP_SEC_ENABLE is set. Verified: CMD18 still reads full 8-sector cluster; no error on Kit/Synth press.
close_reason: Closed

title: M2 — Timers, DMA & system glue
description: Model the time base and data-movement peripherals the firmware relies on: OSTM/MTU2 timers (system fast/slow/superfast ticks), the DMAC used for UART/SSI/SPI chains, GPIO for board signals and jack detect, plus ADC/RTC stubs.
close_reason: All 5 children complete: OSTM, MTU2, DMAC, GPIO timers/data-movement models plus ADC+RTC stubs. The firmware's time base, DMA chains, GPIO board signals, battery sense and RTC all run without faulting.

title: OSTM timer model (0xFCFEC000 / 0xFCFEC400)
description: OSTM timer model (OSTM0 0xFCFEC000, OSTM1 0xFCFEC400). NOW BOOT-BLOCKING after SPIBSC: firmware reads OSTMnCNT (counter, offset 0x04; observed read @0xFCFEC004) in a delay/timing loop, catch-all reads 0 forever. RZA1/ostm/ostm.c getTimerValue reads OSTMnCNT. Regs: CMP 0x00, CNT 0x04, TE 0x10, TS 0x14 (start), TT 0x18 (stop), CTL 0x20 (0=interval/down, mode bits). Minimal unblock: free-running CNT off virtual clock like MTU2 (use ptimer or qemu_clock); honour TS/TT start/stop and CTL up/down mode. Two channels.
close_reason: Implemented OSTM model (src/hw/timer/rza1l_ostm.c): two channels, free-running 32-bit up-counter off virtual clock @33.33MHz; TS/TT start/stop, TE status, interval mode down-counter. Unblocked the scheduler's OSTMnCNT poll; boot advanced 3266->4036 PCs. Commit e62fd90.

title: MTU2 timer model (0xFCFF0000)
description: Model the MTU2 channels (mtu2_iodefine.h) that back the Deluge system timers: FAST 528 kHz (ch2), SLOW 32 kHz (ch4), SUPERFAST 33.792 MHz (ch1), MIDI gate (ch2). Drive the firmware scheduler/tick and raise GIC interrupts.
close_reason: Minimal free-running MTU2 model implemented (src/hw/timer/rza1l_mtu2.c): all TCNT_n + TCNTS advance off the virtual clock (~33.33MHz); writes re-base. Unblocked the firmware boot delay loop. Compare-match/interrupt support deferred until needed (would depend on e5c.5 GIC). Commit e4a9997.

title: DMAC model (0xE8200000)
description: DMAC model (0xE8200000). NOW BOOT-BLOCKING (M1): after MTU2, the firmware starts a DMA transfer and busy-waits on CHSTAT (channel-status, block offset 0x24; observed at 0xE82004A4) for completion forever (catch-all reads 0). Each channel block is 0x40 bytes / 16 u32 regs: N0SA,N0DA,N0TB,N1SA,N1DA,N1TB,CRSA,CRDA,CRTB,CHSTAT(0x24),CHCTRL(0x28),CHCFG(0x2c),CHITVL,CHEXT,NXLA,CRLA. Minimal unblock: on CHCTRL SETEN, perform the N0SA->N0DA copy of N0TB bytes (honour CHCFG size/inc), set CHSTAT END/TC and clear EN. Used by OLED SPI TX (RSPI), SSI audio, SD. Map over io.mid catch-all like rspi0/mtu2.
close_reason: Implemented synchronous DMAC model (src/hw/dma/rza1l_dmac.c): 16 channels, on CHCTRL SETEN copies N0TB bytes N0SA->N0DA honouring SAD/DAD increment + SDS/DDS unit sizes, reports END|TC. DSTAT_* computed from CHSTAT. Unblocked the firmware DMA-completion poll; boot advanced 1079->1458 PCs. Link/repeat/HW-request/IRQ deferred. Commit 7a01199.

title: GPIO model (0xFCFE3004)
description: GPIO model (base 0xFCFE3000; macro GPIO @0xFCFE3004). NOW BOOT-BLOCKING after OSTM: firmware busy-polls PPR1 (port pin read, observed 16-bit read @0xFCFE3204, 120k+ times) waiting on input pin state; catch-all returns 0 forever. Also many config writes seen to PIBC/PBDC/PM/PMC/PFC port regs (e.g. offsets 0x360c/0x3614/0x3a0c/0x720c, 16 each) which are harmless once GPIO accepts them. Struct st_gpio in RZA1/system/iodefines/gpio_iodefine.h: P1..P11 @0x00 (output data, 16b), PSR1..11 @0x44 (32b set/reset), PPR0..11 @0x200 (input pin read, 16b; PPR1@0x204), PM @0x300 (mode), PMC @0x400, PFC @0x500, PFCE @0x600, PIBC @0x4000, PBDC @0x4100, PIPC @0x4200. Minimal unblock: store written port regs in a regs shadow; make PPR_n read back the corresponding P_n output latch (loopback) or a sensible default so the pin-state spin exits. Need to find WHICH pin the boot loop waits for (decode the PC reading 0xFCFE3204) to pick the right return value.
close_reason: Implemented minimal GPIO model (src/hw/gpio/rza1l_gpio.c, struct base 0xFCFE3004): shadow all register writes; PPR_n reads loop back the matching P_n output latch (driven pins read high, undriven inputs read 0). Eliminated the 120k+ PPR1 busy-wait spam; firmware now runs its main input-polling loop (encoders/buttons/detect lines) cleanly. Boot holds at 4036 distinct PCs, zero exceptions. Commit b56e4e6.

title: ADC + RTC stubs
description: Stub the ADC (0xE8005800, battery voltage on P1.13) and RTC (0xFCFF1000) with plausible read values so firmware polling does not fault.
close_reason: ADC (0xE8005800) and RTC (0xFCFF1000) stubbed as sysbus devices under src/hw/misc. ADC: ADCSR always reports conversion-ready, ADDRF returns ~3.7V battery reading so inputRoutine drives the battery LED solid (verified: 33 inputRoutine iterations, 0 batteryLEDBlink, 0 faults). RTC: register file with fixed BCD date/time, accepts writes. No unimplemented ADC/RTC accesses; 0 data aborts with and without SD.

title: M5 — Audio (SSI/I2S)
description: Model SSI0 (I2S, 0xE820B000) wired to QEMU's audio backend for real-time output and input, including the codec config path (RIIC/SCUX) the firmware programs.
close_reason: M5 Audio complete: SSIF I2S register model (d9f.1), audio output (d9f.2), audio input (d9f.3), and codec/SCUX/RIIC investigation (d9f.4, documented as module-standby/unmodelled). Boot healthy with and without audiodev.

title: SSI/I2S device model (0xE820B000)
description: Model SSI0 (ssif_iodefine.h): SSICR/SSIFCR/SSIFTDR/SSIFRDR, I2S framing, 44.1 kHz / 32-bit, TX FIFO fed by DMA ch6 and RX by ch7, with FIFO interrupts to the GIC.
close_reason: Implemented rza1l-ssif register model at 0xE820B000; boot healthy (19/183 IRQs, 0 aborts)

title: Audio output to QEMU audio backend
description: Pipe SSI TX samples to QEMU's audio subsystem for real-time playback; add an audio option to run.sh.
close_reason: SSIF TX ring mirrored to QEMU audio output (44.1kHz stereo S32); run.sh --audio binds audiodev via -global. Boot healthy with/without audio (0 aborts).

title: Audio input path (line/mic)
description: Feed QEMU audio capture (or silence/file) into the SSI RX FIFO so the firmware's input/resampling path runs.
close_reason: SSI RX modelled symmetrically: ch7 CRDA advances from virtual time (input-latency resync works); optional capture voice writes frames into the RX ring at CRDA, silence by default. Boot healthy with/without audio (0 aborts).

title: Audio codec config stub (RIIC/SCUX)
description: Stub the I2C/RIIC codec configuration and SCUX (0xE8208000) registers the firmware writes during audio bring-up so they don't fault.
close_reason: Investigated: firmware holds SCUX (STBCR8) and RIIC0-3 (STBCR9) in module-standby and never accesses them; codec is GPIO-enabled I2S with no I2C config. No stub device needed — documented in register-coverage.md / memory-map.md.

title: M1 — Boot & early SoC init
description: Get the DelugeFirmware image executing on -M deluge: load the image, satisfy the firmware's earliest hardware init (clocks, SDRAM, interrupt controller, L2 cache) so it reaches main loop without faulting, and emit real serial output.
close_reason: M1 complete: all 10 children closed. Firmware loads, runs clocks/SDRAM/INTC/L2 init, and reaches its main input-polling loop with 4036 distinct PCs and zero exceptions. SCIF UART channels wired to -serial backends; firmware drives them with PIC/MIDI binary protocols (no human-readable debug text by design). Peripheral catch-alls (io.low/mid/high) now quiet for the modelled regions.

title: Load firmware image and reach the reset vector
description: Support -kernel for both the ELF and the raw deluge-c1_2_1.bin (load to SRAM 0x20000000 / SDRAM 0x0C000000 per linker_script_rz_a1l.ld; entry symbol 'start'). Confirm the CPU fetches the firmware's first instructions under -d in_asm.
close_reason: Board loads ELF (load_elf) or raw .bin (load_image_targphys -> SRAM 0x20000000) with a reset hook that sets the firmware entry PC. Verified: CPU executes from 0x20000000 and reaches the firmware reset_handler at 0x2007b620 under -d in_asm.

title: SPIBSC SPI multi-I/O flash controller (0x3FEFA000)
description: SPIBSC SPI multi-I/O bus controller @0x3FEFA000 (in io.low window). NOW BOOT-BLOCKING after DMAC: firmware busy-waits on CMNSR (common status, offset 0x48; observed read @0x3FEFA048) for TEND (bit0) on each SPI flash command, catch-all reads 0 forever. Drives the serial NOR/QSPI flash holding firmware+samples; also supports memory-mapped read mode (the 0x18000000 SPI ROM window, RZA1L_SPI_ROM_BASE). Minimal unblock: model CMNSR.TEND always set (transfer complete) + accept SMCR/SMENR/SMCMR/SMADR/SMWDR command regs, optionally returning SMRDR data from a backing flash image. Register order: CMNCR,SSLDR,SPBCR,DRCR,DRCMR,DREAR,DROPR,DRENR,SMCR,SMCMR,SMADR,SMOPR,SMENR,...,CMNSR@0x48.
close_reason: Implemented minimal SPIBSC model (src/hw/ssi/rza1l_spibsc.c): CMNSR.TEND always set, SMRDR reads 0 (flash WIP clear -> busy-wait loops exit), SSLF tracks SMCR.SSLKP/DRCR.SSLN. Unblocked the flash status/command polls; boot advanced 1458->3266 PCs. No backing flash device yet (storage milestone). Commit ee075a7.

title: CPG clock pulse generator model (0xFCFE0010)
description: Model enough of the CPG (cpg_iodefine.h) for the firmware's clock setup (I:G:B:P1:P0 = 400/266/133/66/33 MHz from 13.33 MHz) to complete without faulting; accept FRQCR and standby-clearing writes.
close_reason: CPG modelled (src/hw/misc/rza1l_cpg.c) at 0xFCFE0010: shadows FRQCR/FRQCR2 clock-config and STBCR1..10 module-standby block; CPUSTS reads 0. Accepts firmware clock setup + standby-clearing writes without faulting. Boot 4036 PCs, 0 exceptions. Commit 3adc593.

title: BSC / SDRAM controller model (0x3FFFC000)
description: Model the Bus State Controller (bsc_iodefine.h) so SDRAM init for CS3 (external memory 0x0C000000-0x10000000) succeeds; back it with the SDRAM RAM region created by the SoC.
close_reason: BSC modelled (src/hw/misc/rza1l_bsc.c) at 0x3FFFC000: shadows CSnBCR/CSnWCR/SDCR/RTCSR/RTCOR and the SDRAM mode-set windows (+0x1040/+0x2040). SDRAM backed by existing SoC RAM region at 0x0C000000. io.low catch-all now empty; boot 4036 PCs, 0 exceptions. Commit pending.

title: STB standby + WDT watchdog stubs (0xFCFE0000)
description: Provide module-standby (STB) and watchdog (WDT) register stubs so the firmware can ungate peripheral clocks and kick/refresh the watchdog without faulting.
close_reason: STB standby covered by CPG device (STBCRn live in st_cpg block); WDT modelled separately (src/hw/misc/rza1l_wdt.c) at 0xFCFE0000: keyed WTCSR/WTCNT/WRCSR writes accepted, counter never expires so watchdog refresh loop runs clean. Commit 3adc593.

title: INTC -> Cortex-A9 GIC wiring (0xE8201000)
description: INTC -> Cortex-A9 GIC wiring (INTC 0xE8201000). NEXT FOUNDATIONAL step: firmware reached its main loop (4036 PCs) and now touches INTC during init (writes 0xE8201184/0x201530/0x2011a4 = priority/config regs). The bare cortex-a9 has no GIC; INTC programming hits the io.mid catch-all. To deliver interrupts (timers, SCIF, SSIF, USB) need a GIC. Likely approach: switch the SoC from a raw ARM_CPU cortex-a9 to a9mpcore (TYPE_A9MPCORE_PRIV: built-in GIC + private timers + SCU at PERIPHBASE) OR add a standalone GIC, then map the RZ/A1 INTC registers (st_intc: ICDISER/ICDICER enable, ICDIPR priority, ICDIPTR target, ICCICR/ICCPMR cpu iface) onto the GIC distributor/cpu-iface. RZ/A1 INTC is essentially a PL390/GIC v1 - the register layout may map closely to QEMU's arm_gic. Blocks M2-M7 (any interrupt-driven subsystem). Other scattered post-main-loop accesses (lower priority): SCIF1 UART 0xE8007800, SSIF audio 0xE820B000, USB200 0xE8010000.
close_reason: Wired QEMU arm_gic as the RZ/A1 INTC (GICv1): dist @0xE8201000, cpu iface @0xE8202000, revision 1, 1 cpu, 608 irqs; GIC IRQ/FIQ -> Cortex-A9. Register layout matches so mapped directly (no shim). Firmware INTC init now absorbed by the GIC; boot healthy at 4036 PCs, zero exceptions. Provides interrupt foundation for M2-M7. Commit 4e60219.

title: PL310 L2 cache controller (0x3FFFF000)
description: Instantiate QEMU's PL310 at 0x3FFFF000 (already selected in Kconfig) so cache init writes are absorbed.
close_reason: PL310 instantiated from QEMU built-in l2x0 at 0x3FFFF000 (src/hw/arm/rza1l_soc.c). Firmware L2 cache-init writes absorbed (offset 0x1ff000 gone from io.low catch-all). Boot 4036 PCs, 0 exceptions.

title: SCIF UART model with real serial I/O
description: Flesh out the SCIF stub (scif_iodefine.h; SCIF0 0xE8007000 .. SCIF7 0xE800A800) into a working 8-channel UART: FIFO TX/RX, status/control regs, baud, RX/TX interrupts to the GIC, and a CharFrontend backend so early firmware debug appears on -serial. Channel 0 = MIDI, channel 1 = PIC.
close_reason: SCIF stub now a working single-channel UART (src/hw/char/rza1l_scif.c): sync TX to chardev (TDFE|TEND always ready), interrupt-driven RX (RDF|DR + RXI on RIE). Channels 0(MIDI)/1(PIC) instantiated at 0xE8007000/0xE8007800, wired to -serial, RXI->GIC SPI 191/195. All SCIF accesses absorbed; boot 4036 PCs, 0 exceptions. Commit 61f2108.

title: RSPI0 model (OLED/CV SPI, 0xE800C800)
close_reason: Implemented minimal always-ready RSPI0 model (src/hw/ssi/rza1l_rspi.c): reports SPSR.SPTEF|TEND set, accepts+discards SPDR writes, shadows config regs. Unblocked the firmware OLED/CV SPI busy-wait. Actual OLED pixel/CV DAC output via SPI+DMA deferred to M4. Commit 1e5f283.

title: SoC memory map: uncached mirrors + peripheral catch-alls
close_reason: rza1l_soc.c now models the RZ/A1 +0x40000000 uncached RAM mirrors (SRAM 0x60000000, SDRAM 0x4C000000) as aliases and installs three create_unimplemented_device catch-alls (io.low 0x3FE00000, io.mid 0xE8000000, io.high 0xFCFE0000). Eliminated all early data aborts; firmware runs 1000+ distinct PCs and every peripheral poke is observable via -d unimp. Commit fd4d2f1.

title: Boot stalls in audio/SPIBSC init before PIC firmware-version query
description: Firmware never reaches PIC::requestFirmwareVersion() (deluge.cpp:784) within 25s of boot; it loops earlier in AudioEngine::init / audioFileManager.init / initSPIBSC (deluge.cpp:767-781). Only the first PIC flush (10 config bytes incl SET_UART_SPEED 225) reaches the PIC over SCIF1. Consequence: the nj4.1 PIC firmware-version handshake (245 -> [245,version]) is implemented and the DMA RX-ring delivery primitive is unit-correct, but cannot be exercised end-to-end until this init path completes. Boot coverage is 4036 distinct PCs / 0 exceptions (unchanged from pre-PIC baseline). Investigate which init (SSIF/codec, SD/audioFileManager, or SPIBSC flash backing) spins, likely a status poll on an unmodelled peripheral. Blocks validating nj4.x PIC input/version paths.
close_reason: DMAC now raises a per-channel transfer-end interrupt (edge pulse) on completion when CHCFG.DEM is clear, wired to the GIC at SPI 41+ch-32. The firmware's PIC TX-complete ISR (PIC_TX_INT_TrnEnd -> tx_interrupt) now runs and clears txSending, so the UART TX queue drains. requestFirmwareVersion's byte 245 now reaches the PIC and the version handshake completes; PIC stream advanced from ~10 to 499 bytes, boot 4036->4069 distinct PCs, 0->5 IRQ deliveries.

title: [BUG] SCALE button only shows scale, never toggles scale mode on/off
description: In the QEMU 'deluge' machine the SCALE/Type button (firmware coord SCALE_MODE, skin entry at 1208,514) only exhibits its press/hold behaviour — it shows/flashes the current scale and root note — but a normal short press never toggles scale mode on/off. Expected: hold = show current scale; short press = toggle scale mode on/off.
acceptance_criteria: From an instrument clip in the QEMU deluge machine: a SHORT press/click of the SCALE button toggles scale mode on and off (SCALE_MODE indicator LED and clip->inScaleMode flip each short press), while HOLDING the button still shows/flashes the current scale and root note. Verified with both the 'm' key binding and a skin mouse click at the SCALE button centre.
notes:
Firmware logic lives in InstrumentClipView::handleScaleButtonAction() (firmware2/src/deluge/gui/views/instrument_clip_view.cpp ~L855-920). On press it sets UI_MODE_SCALE_MODE_BUTTON_PRESSED, toggleScaleModeOnButtonRelease=true, records scaleButtonPressTime, and calls commandFlashRootNote() / displayCurrentRootNoteAndScaleName() (the 'show scale' behaviour). On RELEASE it only toggles when 'toggleScaleModeOnButtonRelease && isShortPress(scaleButtonPressTime)' is true; otherwise it falls through to the long-press branch (just shows the scale name). So the symptom = every press is being classified as a LONG press.

isShortPress() (firmware2/src/deluge/deluge.cpp:233) compares elapsed AudioEngine::audioSampleTimer samples against kShortPressTime (= kSampleRate/2, ~0.5s). Candidate root causes to investigate, in order:
1. audioSampleTimer not advancing at real sample rate in the emulator (or jumping), so the sample delta between press and release exceeds kShortPressTime even for a quick click -> always long press.
2. Host key auto-repeat / the input layer holding the button: a held host key resends key-down events; for mouse, DELUGE_INPUT_MIN_PRESS_MS=120 in src/hw/input/deluge_input.c enforces a 120ms floor (fine vs 0.5s, so probably not the cause for clicks).
3. Button release event not delivered promptly / press and release coalesced.

Repro: enter an instrument clip (non-KIT), press SCALE briefly via the 'm' key binding or a skin click at (1208,514); observe the SCALE LED / inScaleMode never toggles, only the scale popup shows.
close_reason: Closed

title: Investigate and add USB power LED (approx 254,44px)
description: There is an LED centered near pixel coordinates 254,44 that appears to be for USB power (and possibly USB activity). It may not be digitally controlled — it could be part of the power circuitry — but if it is GPIO-driven it should be added to the emulator skin and LED state tracking. Research the Deluge schematic/firmware to determine if this LED is software-controllable, and if so, wire it up.

title: M4 — Input PIC & display
description: Model the Deluge PIC coprocessor (SCIF1 serial link) that scans the pad/button/encoder matrix and drives the RGB pad LEDs, gold-knob indicators, 7-segment display and the 128x48 OLED. Decode CPU->PIC commands, synthesize PIC->CPU input events, and render the displays to a QEMU console.
close_reason: Closed

title: PIC coprocessor device on SCIF1 (link + framing)
description: Create the PIC model attached to SCIF1 (initial 31250 bps, full-speed 200000 bps; DMA TX10/RX12). Implement the byte-stream framing, baud switch (cmd 225) and firmware-version query (245). Refs: deluge/drivers/pic/pic.h.
close_reason: PIC modelled as a chardev backend on SCIF1; CPU->PIC bytes flow via TX DMA+SCIF, PIC->CPU via a new DMAC receive-ring push primitive on ch12. Byte-stream framing skeleton with SET_UART_SPEED(225) baud-switch and REQUEST_FIRMWARE_VERSION(245) response implemented. Boot 4036 PCs/0 exceptions, smoke OK. Full per-command framing=nj4.2. Version handshake unreachable end-to-end until delugemu-hv9 (audio/SPIBSC init stall) is fixed.

title: Decode CPU->PIC command stream
description: Decode outbound PIC commands: pad colours (1-9, 48B), per-LED on/off (188-215 / 152-179), gold-knob indicators (20-21), 7-seg update (224), scroll buffers (228-242), dimmer/timing (243-244), and OLED control (247-251 ENABLE/SELECT/DC). Maintain LED/display state for the renderers.
close_reason: deluge_pic.c decodes the full CPU->PIC command set with correct per-command payload lengths, keeping the stream framed for all commands. Maintains display state for the renderers: pad_grid[18][8] RGB, led_on[36], seven_seg[4], gold_knob[2][4], and OLED enable/select/DC control lines. Verified end-to-end against the firmware2 debug ELF (colour blocks, 7-seg, gold knobs and the version handshake all frame correctly; 0 aborts; boot reaches the main loop and OLED rendering). Old blob unchanged.

title: Synthesize PIC->CPU input events
description: Generate inbound events: pad/button press (index 0-179), release (252 + index), boot/reset (129/175), firmware-version (245+byte), idle heartbeat (254). Model the 8x16 pad + 4x9 button matrices and 6 encoders. Refs: deluge/hid.
close_reason: Closed

title: Host input mapping (keyboard/gamepad -> events)
description: Map host keyboard/gamepad input to pad/button/encoder events fed into the PIC encoder, so the firmware is controllable headlessly before the graphical skin exists.
close_reason: Closed

title: OLED display device (128x48 SSD130x) to console
description: Render the OLED (SSD130x command set, 128x48, top pixel offset 5) driven via RSPI0 through the PIC DC/SELECT lines. Maintain a framebuffer and draw to a QEMU graphic console. Refs: RZA1/oled, deluge/drivers/oled.
close_reason: OLED 128x48 renders to QEMU graphic console; whole pipeline working end-to-end

title: 7-segment numeric display rendering
description: Render the 4-char numeric display updated via PIC cmd 224 (seven_segment.h) as an alternative/secondary console readout.
close_reason: Closed

title: RGB pad-grid LED rendering
description: Render the 8x16 main pad grid + 2x8 sidebar RGB LEDs (144 LEDs) from decoded PIC colour/LED state to a console, as the headless precursor to the graphical skin.
close_reason: Closed

title: M9 — Host I/O integration (real audio, real MIDI, playable UX)
description: Make the emulator genuinely usable as an instrument: route the modelled SSI audio to the host's system audio (so you actually hear the Deluge and can sample into it), bridge the DIN-MIDI (SCIF0) and USB-MIDI chardevs to real host MIDI ports, and finish the interactive front-panel skin (tracked under M6). The device models for audio (M5) and MIDI/USB (M7) already exist and expose QEMU backends; this epic is the host-side glue, defaults, and end-to-end verification that turn those into a plug-and-play experience on macOS and Windows.

title: Route SSI audio to host system audio (CoreAudio/WASAPI)
description: The SSIF model (delugemu-d9f) already streams the firmware's I2S TX DMA ring to a QEMU audio voice via DEFINE_AUDIO_PROPERTIES(audiodev=). Wire it up so the user actually hears the Deluge on the host: pick/default a host audiodev (coreaudio on macOS, dsound/wasapi on Windows, pa/sdl on Linux), bind it to rza1l-ssif via -audiodev + audiodev= (or a board default), and surface it through scripts/run.sh --audio. Verify end-to-end audible output (play a note/clip and confirm sound), check for underruns/glitches at 44.1kHz stereo, and document the recommended invocation per OS. Acceptance: a one-flag run.sh invocation produces clean audible playback on macOS.
acceptance_criteria: run.sh --audio coreaudio (and equivalent) yields clean, audible 44.1kHz stereo output from the firmware; no persistent underruns; documented per-OS.
close_reason: Audio routing verified end-to-end. SSIF I2S TX ring already streams to a QEMU audio voice (DEFINE_AUDIO_PROPERTIES audiodev=). scripts/run.sh --audio now accepts a driver name or 'auto' (coreaudio/macOS, pa/Linux, dsound/Windows) and binds it via -audiodev id=deluge0 + -global rza1l-ssif.audiodev=deluge0. Verified with the wav backend: auditioning 8 notes produced non-silent 44.1kHz 16-bit stereo PCM (peak 0.20 FS), no underruns/xruns in log; the only audio log line was the harmless output-only-backend 'ssif.in (no host audio driver)'. coreaudio output voice opens cleanly (ssif.out, no error); its capture voice is unsupported in this build and degrades gracefully (firmware sees silent line-in). README + run.sh help document the per-OS invocation.

title: Capture host audio into the SSI input (line/mic sampling)
description: The SSIF receive path + audio input voice (delugemu-d9f.3) lets the firmware read samples from a QEMU capture voice. Wire it to a real host capture device so the Deluge's sampler / LINE IN / MIC can record from system audio or an input device. Bind a capture-capable audiodev to rza1l-ssif (audiodev= with in.*), expose it via run.sh, and verify a resample/record on the firmware captures host audio. Note the front panel's LINE IN, GAIN and MIC inputs map here. Acceptance: firmware resample records recognisable host audio through a documented run.sh flag.
acceptance_criteria: A documented run.sh flag lets the firmware's sampler capture host audio (line/mic) end-to-end.

title: Bridge DIN + USB MIDI chardevs to real host MIDI ports
description: Both MIDI transports are modelled and exposed as QEMU chardevs: DIN MIDI on SCIF0 (serial_hd(0) / run.sh --midi) and USB-MIDI on rza1l-usb (serial_hd(1) / run.sh --usb-midi). QEMU has no native CoreMIDI/WinMM chardev backend, so connecting to real host MIDI needs a bridge. Provide a small host-side bridge (e.g. a helper script/process that connects a QEMU PTY or TCP/UDP socket chardev to a CoreMIDI virtual port on macOS / WinMM or loopMIDI on Windows / ALSA seq on Linux), or document a reliable existing-tool path. Cover both directions (in and out) and both transports (DIN + USB-MIDI). Verify notes/CC round-trip with a real DAW or MIDI controller, and surface it through run.sh. Acceptance: a documented one-command setup makes the emulated Deluge appear as a usable MIDI in/out device to host software.
acceptance_criteria: Documented bridge exposes the Deluge's DIN and USB MIDI as real host MIDI in/out ports; notes/CC round-trip with host MIDI software is verified.

title: Emulate rotary-encoder turns via GPIO quadrature + IRQn
description: Unlike pads/buttons (which go through the PIC), the Deluge's seven rotary encoders are read directly by the RZ/A1L: each encoder has an A-side pin routed via PFC alt-2 to an external IRQn and a B-side companion pin read as plain GPIO inside the ISR (firmware hid/encoders.cpp). Encoder map (port 1 pins / IRQn): SCROLL_Y irq8/comp10/IRQ0, SCROLL_X irq11/comp12/IRQ3, TEMPO irq6/comp7/IRQ2(inv), SELECT irq3/comp2/IRQ7(inv), MOD_1 irq5/comp4/IRQ1, MOD_0 irq0/comp15/IRQ4. To make the gold/black encoders usable, the GPIO model must drive PPR1 pin states for a host-driven quadrature step and the external IRQn lines must be wired into the INTC->GIC so the firmware's encoder ISR fires and reads direction from the companion pin. Add a host API (deluge_gpio_encoder_step(name, dir)) the input layer / skin calls on scroll-wheel or drag, generating a correct A/B quadrature transition + IRQn pulse. Encoder push-clicks already work via the PIC button matrix. Acceptance: turning an encoder from the host moves the corresponding firmware value/selection (e.g. SELECT scrolls a menu, TEMPO changes BPM).
acceptance_criteria: Host-driven encoder steps produce correct firmware response (SELECT scrolls menus, TEMPO changes BPM, MOD knobs change values) via GPIO quadrature + IRQn.
notes: Encoder feel: the two GOLD encoders (MOD_0, MOD_1) are CONTINUOUS/endless rotaries — free-spinning, no detents, report relative motion indefinitely; map host scroll/drag to a steady stream of quadrature steps. The four BLACK encoders (SELECT, TEMPO, SCROLL_X, SCROLL_Y) are INCREMENTAL/detented — one quadrature cycle per physical click/detent; map host scroll notches 1:1 to single detent steps for a tactile feel. (The 3rd gold encoder = main VOLUME, not clickable and not on the MCU encoder/IRQn path — likely analog, out of scope for this task.) This distinction matters for host UX: detented black encoders should advance one menu item / one grid step per wheel notch; continuous gold encoders should scale smoothly with drag velocity.
close_reason: Implemented host-driven encoder API (deluge GPIO quadrature + IRQn). All acceptance examples visually verified on QEMU: SELECT scrolls settings menu (navigates through distinct frames), TEMPO changes BPM (120->127), MOD gold knobs change values (LPF popup). Also fixed an RZ/A1 DMAC chained dual-register (Next0/Next1, CHCFG REN|RSW|DEM) transfer-completion bug that was wedging firmware->PIC UART TX (txSending stuck) and freezing the OLED in menus.

title: Verify/fix PIC pad+button input reaches firmware actions
description: During the USB-MIDI session, host key events were observed reaching deluge_input_event and deluge_pic_button_event (correct pad/button indices, press+release, PIC pointer set), yet the firmware appeared not to act on them (OLED unchanged for PLAY/LOAD/KEYBOARD). This may be a real delivery gap (PIC->CPU event framing, SCIF1 RX DMA ring timing, or held_count/NEXT_PAD_OFF sequencing) or a test-methodology artifact (those buttons may not visibly change the OLED in that state). This blocks a playable UI: pads and named buttons must reliably trigger firmware actions. Investigate end-to-end (host key -> PIC event bytes -> SCIF1 DMA -> firmware uartGetChar/input processing), add a deterministic assertion (e.g. pressing a pad changes pad-grid LEDs, or KEYBOARD view toggles a known OLED/LED state), and fix any gap. Blocks delugemu-7z3.2 (map skin controls to PIC input).
acceptance_criteria: A scripted host pad/button press produces a deterministic, observable firmware reaction (LED/OLED/state change); regression test added.
close_reason: Verified end-to-end: scripted pad press produces deterministic firmware reaction (audition column lights while held, clears on release). Hit-test/press-release/held-tracking fixes committed in 6a3bc49; regression test tests/pad-click.sh present. Session-view main grid is a clip launcher (hardware-accurate), not a step toggle.

title: Pass host USB devices through to the Deluge USB host port
description: delugemu-6ky/3sn model a SYNTHETIC USB-MIDI device attached to the rza1l-usb host controller and bridge its bulk pipes to a host MIDI chardev. This task is the complementary capability: let a REAL USB device on the host machine appear on the emulated Deluge USB host port, so e.g. a class-compliant USB-MIDI controller or USB mass-storage stick plugged into the host enumerates and works inside the firmware. Approach options to evaluate: (a) QEMU usb-host passthrough (libusb) attached to the rza1l-usb roothub if we expose a standard USB bus, or (b) extend rza1l_usb.c to proxy enumeration/transfers for an arbitrary backing device rather than the hard-coded MIDI descriptor set. Covers USB-MIDI controllers first; USB mass-storage is a stretch goal.
acceptance_criteria: A real class-compliant USB device attached on the host (start with a USB-MIDI controller) enumerates through the rza1l-usb model and is usable by the firmware (MIDI in/out flows), with a documented run.sh flag to select the host device.
notes: Build on the enumeration + control-transfer + bulk-pipe machinery from 6ky/3sn (CFIFO/CURPIPE, BRDY/BEMP, SYSSTS0 attach). Decide between QEMU usb-host(libusb) passthrough vs proxying through rza1l_usb. Host USB permissions differ on macOS vs Linux.
